div.div: ALPHALIZE
POS SUITE FOR ODOO 19
POS DYNAMIC INVOICE
Invoice Settings - User Guide
Set up your branded receipt, add paper sizes, and design the invoice layout that prints - a simple, step-by-step guide to the three screens under Invoice Settings.
Prepared for

Everything lives under Point of Sale > Invoice Settings, which holds three screens: General Settings, Receipt Paper Sizes and Invoice Layouts. Set them up once and every receipt follows. Each screen is explained below.
PART 1 General Settings
This is the main screen: choose how the receipt looks, its size, and your branding.
Standard = plain receipt. Dynamic / Cash Memo = ready-made branded layouts. Custom Layout (editable) = design your own in the visual editor (Part 3). Whatever you pick here is what the app prints.
Click Preview (top of the form) to see the selected template rendered against your most recent order - exactly what will print.
Turn on Use Default Receipt Size and pick a size (e.g. 4 inch). Add or edit sizes on the Receipt Paper Sizes screen (Part 2).
Company name (English + Arabic), C.R. number, GSM, VAT and logo appear on the receipt. Each line has a Show toggle so you control what prints.
TIP - With Custom Layout (editable) selected, an Edit Layout (Drag & Drop) button appears; it opens the visual editor for the current size.
PART 2 Receipt Paper Sizes
Manage every paper size your shop prints on. The built-in sizes are here, and you can add your own. Each size you add automatically gets its own layout under Invoice Layouts.

div.span: Add a size the easy way

div.div: Click New, then in Name type just a number - e.g. 6. It becomes "6 inch" automatically and the Width is filled for you (1 inch ~ 25.4 mm). You can still adjust the width.

tr.td: Shown in the size picker (e.g. "6 inch").
A stable code auto-filled from the name. Don't change it once the size is in use.
The paper width. 3 inch ~ 76 mm, 4 inch ~ 100 mm, A4 = 210 mm.
0 = continuous roll (auto height). A positive number pins a fixed page height.

div.div: NOTE - Very narrow sizes (2 inch / 50 mm) are tight for a full bilingual table; 3 inch and up give the cleanest result.
PART 3 Invoice Layouts (Visual Editor)
Design exactly what the receipt shows. There is one layout per paper size, so each size can look different.
Open a layout (or click Edit Layout in General Settings). You'll see three panels: Design (your sections), Options (edit the selected section), and Live Preview (exactly what prints).
Sections stack top-to-bottom and the paper grows to fit - more company details or more products simply make it longer. List mode prints exactly like the editor at every size.
TIP - Grid mode lets you drag freely, but fixed positions can shift when printed at a different width. For print that always matches the editor, stay in List.

Click any section to edit it on the right: change the Title text (English + Arabic), the Grand Total label, company details, alignment and width; drag to reorder; use the eye icon to hide/show. Company Name (English and Arabic) are their own sections you can place separately, and the Company Header fields can be reordered and added to.
Every change auto-saves (the Save button just confirms). To make a layout the one that prints, pick Custom Layout (editable) in General Settings.
> Quick Reference

tr.td: Pick the invoice template, default size, and branding; Preview and Edit Layout.
Add/edit the sizes you print on (type a number = "N inch").
Design each size's receipt in the visual editor (List = print-exact).
See the current template against your latest order.
Layout changes save instantly; the Live Preview is what prints.

div.div: > Things to Remember

ul.li: Choose the Invoice Template and Default Receipt Size first - everything else builds on them.
To add a size, just type a number in Name; the width fills automatically, and a layout is created for it.
Height 0 = continuous roll; a number = fixed page height.
Design in List mode so the printed receipt always matches the editor and grows with content.
Layout edits auto-save; the Live Preview is exactly what prints.
To use your designed layout, select Custom Layout (editable) in General Settings.

div.div:
- End of Guide -
Alphalize | POS Dynamic Invoice - User Manual
